records.ety: [A dim., fr. the root of cringe; akin to D. krinkelen to wind or twist. Cf. Cringle, Cringe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crinkled (-k'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Crinkling (-klng).]

defs: To form with short turns, bends, or wrinkles; to mold into inequalities or sinuosities; to cause to wrinkle or curl.
quotes: The houses crinkled to and fro.
Her face all bowsy, Comely crinkled, Wondrously wrinkled.
The flames through all the casements pushing forth, Like red-not devils crinkled into snakes.

defs: To turn or wind; to run in and out in many short bends or turns; to curl; to run in waves; to wrinkle; also, to rustle, as stiff cloth when moved.
quotes: The green wheat crinkles like a lake.
And all the rooms Were full of crinkling silks.

defs: A winding or turn; wrinkle; sinuosity.
quotes: The crinkles in this glass, making objects appear double.
